description: MTB suspension air pressure reference data for RockShox, Fox, DVO, and Öhlins forks and rear shocks. Pressure charts are based on manufacturer guidelines and community-verified data. All weights are in kg, pressures in PSI.
usage_notes: pressure_chart entries define inclusive weight ranges and the corresponding PSI range
Interpolate linearly within a weight range for a more precise value
If formula_psi is present, use it as the primary starting pressure instead of the chart
Always cycle suspension 5-10 times after pressurising to equalise positive/negative chambers
Add ~5-10 PSI per volume spacer/token added (reduces available air volume)
Measure sag with rider seated in normal riding position, wearing full gear
secondary_chamber field (Öhlins, DVO Topaz): some models require a second valve to be pressurised separately — see that field's notes
Öhlins RXF forks use a 3-chamber system: main + negative + ramp-up. Ramp-up runs ~1.5–2× main pressure and controls mid-stroke and bottom-out support
DVO forks use OTT (Off-The-Top) external preload to tune small-bump sensitivity independently from air pressure

forks.setup_notes: Dual Air system: set negative chamber 5-15 PSI below positive. Pump positive first, then negative.

rebound.note: Turn red knob clockwise (in) to slow rebound. XC fork — run faster than trail equivalents.

hsc.note: Clockwise = more high-speed compression damping.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

forks.setup_notes: Solo Air spring: single valve pressurises both chambers simultaneously.

rebound.note: 6-click rebound dial. Counter-clockwise = faster rebound.

compression.label: Open / Trail / Climb
compression.note: 3-position platform lever. Open = plush. Trail = pedalling support. Climb = firm lockout.

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

lsc.note: 11-click LSC. GRIP damper. FIT4 (Factory) uses 3-position lever instead.

rebound_hsr.note: HSR (High Speed Rebound). Controls rebound on fast hits. Start at 3 clicks; adjust ±1.

hsc.note: HSC (High Speed Compression). Controls harsh hits/drops. Start at 2–3 clicks.

rebound_lsr.note: LSR (Low Speed Rebound). Red dial. 16 clicks.

rebound_lsr.note: Turn clockwise to slow rebound. Counter-clockwise = faster.

dvo.brand_notes: DVO Suspension uses an air positive spring paired with a steel coil negative spring on all forks. This means lower air pressures than most competitors. The OTT (Off-The-Top) external adjuster tunes small-bump sensitivity independently from air pressure. DVO shocks feature a bladder IFP design that separates oil from air for improved small-bump compliance.

forks.air_spring: Positive air + steel coil negative spring
forks.setup_notes: Positive air + steel coil negative spring. No volume spacers — tune progression via oil volume above air piston (requires service). OTT external adjuster (14 rotations total, 6 clicks per rotation) tunes first ~30mm of travel independently. Known pressure points: 54 kg → 55 PSI, 68 kg → 85 PSI, 81.6 kg → 105 PSI, 95 kg → 125 PSI, 108 kg → 135 PSI.

rebound.note: 20-click rebound adjuster. Fully closed (all in) = slowest. Back out for faster rebound.

hsc.note: 29-position HSC (counted in half-turns). Main compression adjuster on DVO forks. Clockwise = more damping.

lsc.note: 6-position LSC dial (D1 SL only). Base D1 has no external LSC.

ott.note: OTT (Off-The-Top) external coil preload. Adjusts negative spring independently — does NOT change air pressure. More preload = more initial resistance / less sag in first ~30mm. Start at 3–5 rotations from fully unwound. Fine-tune in 1-rotation increments.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

forks.air_spring: Positive air + steel coil negative spring
forks.setup_notes: 36mm stanchion trail/enduro fork. Positive air + coil negative spring system — runs lower PSI than most competitors at same sag. OTT (Off-The-Top): 6 clicks = 1 rotation, 14 total rotations of preload. D1 SL variant adds high/low-speed compression and rebound damping controls. No traditional volume spacers; progression tuned by oil volume inside the air piston cavity (service required).

rebound.note: 20-click rebound. Same damper as Sapphire. Back out from fully closed for faster rebound.

hsc.note: 29-position HSC (half-turns). Primary compression adjuster. Start near middle of range.

ott.note: OTT preload adjuster. Tunes small-bump sensitivity in first 30mm of travel independently from air pressure. 14 rotations total. Start at 3–5 rotations from fully unwound and adjust to preference.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

forks.air_spring: Positive air + steel coil negative spring
forks.setup_notes: 38mm stanchion enduro fork. Same air + coil negative spring architecture as Diamond. OTT external adjuster for independent small-bump tuning. D1 SL variant includes high/low-speed compression and rebound. Travel adjustable 160–180mm via air spring shaft swap (service required).

rebound.note: 25-click rebound. Larger stanchion — heavier damping range. Start near middle.

hsc.note: HSC adjusted in half-turn increments (~10 half-turns total). Clockwise = more compression.

ott.note: OTT preload. Same setup as Diamond/Sapphire. Start 3–5 rotations from fully unwound.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

forks.air_spring: Positive air + steel coil negative spring
forks.setup_notes: DH-specific fork with twin-tube open-bath damping. OTT tunes first 70mm of travel (15 total rotations, 6 clicks per rotation). Adjusts high/low-speed compression, rebound, air and OTT independently. Air pressure range is 60–100 PSI — much lower than most DH forks.

rebound.note: 40-click rebound adjuster. DH fork — wide range for big-hit control. Start near 30–40% of range.

hsc.note: 33-click HSC. Start near middle; adjust based on harshness on large impacts.

lsc.note: 27-click LSC. Controls pedal platform feel and mid-speed hits.

ott.note: OTT preload. 15 rotations on Emerald DH. Tunes first 70mm of travel independently. Start at 4–6 rotations from fully unwound.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.volume_spacer_note: Quick-clip spacers for positive AND negative chambers independently. Up to 2 spacers in each chamber.

secondary_chamber.name: Bladder
secondary_chamber.note: Set bladder pressure to 140–180 PSI separately from main air spring. Bladder separates oil from air for better small-bump sensitivity. Check with separate shock pump before each ride.

shocks.setup_notes: Dual-chamber design with bladder IFP. Main spring: starting point is rider weight in lbs ≈ PSI. Bladder MUST be set separately to 140–180 PSI. T3 compression adjustment (3 quick positions). 9-click rebound. Add positive chamber spacers if bottoming out; add negative chamber spacers to raise BB height without losing mid-stroke feel.

rebound.note: 9-click rebound adjuster. Compact shock — each click is significant. Start at 4–5 clicks.

compression.label: Open / Trail / Firm
compression.note: T3 3-position compression lever. Open = most travel/plush. Trail = pedalling support. Firm = near-lockout.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

secondary_chamber.name: Bladder
secondary_chamber.note: Maintain bladder pressure at 170–200 PSI for best performance and durability. Set separately from main air spring.

shocks.setup_notes: Lightweight XC/trail shock. Main air: rider weight lbs ≈ PSI starting point. Bladder must be maintained at 170–200 PSI separately. Jade X variant adds T3 compression. Also available as Jade Coil (separate model with coil spring, no air).

rebound.note: 9-click rebound. Start near middle (4–5 clicks). Adjust 1 click at a time.

compression.label: Open / Trail / Firm
compression.note: T3 3-position compression lever on Jade X variant. Standard Jade: single compression adjuster.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.setup_notes: Coil shock version of the Jade — no air pressure. Select spring rate by rider weight. Bladder IFP still present for oil/gas separation in damper. Consult DVO spring rate chart for correct spring selection.

damping.note: Coil shock. Rebound dial present (continuous sweep, not click-based). Adjust until rebound feels controlled without harsh kickback.

ohlins.brand_notes: Öhlins MTB forks use a patented three-chamber air spring system: a main chamber (controls spring rate / sag), a negative chamber (auto-equalised on each stroke), and a ramp-up chamber (controls mid-stroke support and bottom-out resistance, set separately at ~1.5–2× main pressure). This makes setup slightly more involved than single-chamber forks but allows very precise tuning. Öhlins shocks use the TTX twin-tube damper technology. All pressures shown are for the main chamber unless noted.

secondary_chamber.name: Ramp-up
secondary_chamber.note: Set ramp-up chamber to approximately 1.5–2× main pressure. For 90 PSI main → set ramp-up to ~150–170 PSI. Ramp-up controls mid-stroke support and bottom-out resistance. Adjust separately via ramp-up valve (smaller Schrader valve, usually gold cap).

forks.setup_notes: Sag target is lower than most brands (10–15%) due to XC focus. Main chamber controls sag; ramp-up controls progression (set to 1.5–2× main). Token system (m.2 version) adjusts volume in main chamber. Known data: 60 kg → 90 PSI main / 170 PSI ramp-up; 70 kg → 100 PSI main / 180 PSI ramp-up; 88 kg → 90–100 PSI main. Pressure chart often printed on lower leg sticker.

rebound.note: 15-click rebound. Count from fully closed. Start near middle (6–8 clicks). Turn clockwise = slower.

lsc.note: 15-click LSC. Controls mid-speed compression. Start near middle. Clockwise = more damping.

hsc.label: Open / Position 1 / Position 2 / Climb
hsc.note: 4-position compression lever (3 riding positions + climb/lockout). Start at Position 1 for most riders. Move to Position 2 for more compression on technical/rough terrain.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

secondary_chamber.name: Ramp-up
secondary_chamber.note: Set ramp-up chamber to approximately 1.4–1.7× main pressure. For 100 PSI main → set ramp-up to ~140–160 PSI. Ramp-up valve has a gold dust cap on most versions. Adjust to prevent harsh bottom-out without losing supple feel.

forks.setup_notes: 36mm stanchion for trail/enduro use. Three-chamber system: set main for sag, then set ramp-up to ~1.5× main. Known data: 71 kg → 85 PSI main / 140 PSI ramp-up; 85–86 kg → 115 PSI main / 160 PSI ramp-up. Token system for adding progression. Negative chamber equalises automatically each stroke. Pressure chart printed on lower leg.

rebound.note: 15-click rebound. Start at 6–8 clicks for most riders. Adjust 1–2 clicks at a time.

lsc.note: 15-click LSC. Clockwise = more compression. Run near middle of range for trail use.

hsc.label: Open / Position 1 / Position 2 / Climb
hsc.note: 4-position compression lever (3 riding positions + climb/lockout). Start at Position 1 for most riders. Move to Position 2 for more compression on technical/rough terrain.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

secondary_chamber.name: Ramp-up
secondary_chamber.note: Set ramp-up chamber to approximately 1.6–1.9× main pressure. For 120 PSI main → set ramp-up to ~190–215 PSI. Higher ramp-up factor than RXF 36 due to longer travel. Known baseline: 120 PSI main / 210 PSI ramp-up for ~85 kg rider.

forks.setup_notes: Flagship enduro fork. Three-chamber system: main sets sag (25–30%), ramp-up at ~1.75× main (e.g. 120 PSI main → 210 PSI ramp-up). Sag range: 42–51mm at 170mm travel. m.2 and m.3 variants available — m.3 adds updated damper internals. 15 clicks LSC, 15 clicks rebound, 3 clicks HSC. Always set main first, then ramp-up.

rebound.note: 15-click rebound. Enduro use — heavier riders and rougher terrain may need more clicks than RXF 36.

lsc.note: 15-click LSC. Start slightly higher than RXF 36 for enduro terrain.

hsc.label: Open / Position 1 / Position 2 / Climb
hsc.note: 4-position compression lever (3 riding positions + climb/lockout). Start at Position 1 for most riders. Move to Position 2 for more compression on technical/rough terrain.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.volume_spacer_note: Band spacers fit in eyelet end; one puck-style spacer fits on shaft. Reduces air volume = increases progression. Use to prevent bottoming.
shocks.setup_notes: Single-tube TTX design for linear suspension kinematics. Starting pressure: rider weight in lbs ≈ PSI. Max 300 PSI. Volume spacers available in 2–12 cc sizes; band spacers go in eyelet, one puck spacer on shaft. TTX twin-tube damper gives more consistent damping across the stroke than conventional single-tube designs.

rebound.note: 12-click rebound. TTX twin-tube design provides consistent damping. Start at 5–6 clicks.

lsc.note: 10-click LSC. Twin-tube design = more consistent across stroke than single-tube. Clockwise = more.

hsc.label: Open / Medium / Firm
hsc.note: 3-position HSC lever. Open = most compliance. Firm = maximum support. Start at Medium for trail; adjust based on feel.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.volume_spacer_note: Band spacers in eyelet end; puck spacer on shaft. Sizes in cc (cubic centimetres). Reduces air volume = more progressive feel.
shocks.setup_notes: Double-tube TTX design for progressive suspension kinematics (higher leverage ratio bikes). Max 325 PSI — highest of all models in this dataset (suitable for e-MTB and heavier riders). Starting point: weight in lbs ≈ PSI. Known data: 88 kg rider → 202 PSI with 10 cc spacers at 28% sag. After pressurising, pump may read ~10 PSI higher — disconnect slowly. Volume spacers available 2–12 cc.

rebound.note: 12-click rebound. Double-can design. Start at 5–6 clicks and adjust to feel.

hsc.label: Open / Medium / Firm
hsc.note: 3-position HSC lever. Open = most compliance. Firm = maximum support. Start at Medium for trail; adjust based on feel.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.setup_notes: Coil spring shock — no air pressure. Twin-tube TTX damper with coil spring. Spring rate range: 251–708 lb/in (44–124 N/mm) in 23 lb/in increments. For 55–115 kg riders. Damping: 3 clicks HSC, 16 clicks LSC, 7 clicks rebound. Consult Öhlins spring rate guide or your bike manufacturer's recommended rates. Weight ~450 g.

rebound.note: 7-click rebound. Coil shock — fewer clicks than air equivalents. Each click is significant.

lsc.note: 16-click LSC. Coil shocks typically need more LSC to control the livelier spring. Start near middle.

hsc.label: Open / Medium / Firm
hsc.note: 3-position HSC. Coil shock — start at Open or Medium. Move to Firm for DH/drops.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

manitou.brand_notes: Manitou's signature feature is the IRT (Infinite Rate Tune) system — a secondary positive air chamber on most forks that independently controls mid-stroke to end-stroke progression. IRT-equipped forks have TWO Schrader valves: always fill the IRT (upper/top) valve FIRST, then the main (lower/bottom) chamber. Main chamber controls sag and initial feel; IRT controls ramp-up and bottom-out resistance. Starting formula: Main ≈ 1/3 rider weight (lbs), IRT ≈ Main + model-specific offset (30 PSI for Mezzer, ~68 PSI for Mattoc). IVA (Incremental Volume Adjust) spacers are Manitou's integrated token system. TPC/TPC+ dampers provide Twin Piston Control for independent HSC/LSC tuning. Manitou shocks run lower starting pressures than Fox/RockShox — typically 66–70% of body weight in lbs rather than 100%.

forks.setup_notes: XC fork with ISO air single-valve system. Negative spring self-equalises automatically. Wheel should be off the ground when pressurising for accurate sag measurement. Kwik-Toggle lockout on Comp/Expert. 32mm stanchions, HexLock SL thru-axle. Travel adjustable in 10mm steps via internal spacer.

rebound.note: Continuous sweep rebound dial — not click-based. Turn until rebound feels controlled without harsh kickback. Roughly 180° of rotation. Start at centre position.

compression.label: Open / Trail / Climb
compression.note: Kwik-Toggle 3-position: Open (full travel), Trail (pedalling support), Climb (near-lockout). Use Trail for most XC riding.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

forks.setup_notes: 32mm stanchion trail fork. TS Air self-equalising negative spring: single valve, automatically pressurises negative chamber on each compression stroke. Kwik-Toggle damper with external lockout. HexLock SL thru-axle. Travel adjustable 80–100mm or 100–120mm via internal spacer. Community starting point: ~40–50 PSI for average adult rider. No IRT system.

rebound.note: Continuous sweep rebound dial (not click-based). Start at centre. Adjust until rebound is just controlled — not kicking back harshly.

compression.label: Open / Trail / Climb
compression.note: Kwik-Toggle 3-position lever. Trail position suits most riding. Climb for sustained ascents.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

forks.setup_notes: 34mm stanchion fat bike and plus-tyre fork. Single valve pressurises both chambers simultaneously. Run 10–15 PSI below normal recommendation to account for extra air volume absorbed by fat/plus tyres. 27.5+ (48mm offset) and 29+ (51mm offset) variants available. No IRT system.

rebound.note: Sweep rebound dial. Fat/plus bikes add inertia — run slightly more rebound damping than on a standard tyre bike. Start slightly past centre toward slower.

compression.label: Open / Trail / Climb
compression.note: 3-position compression. Open recommended for most fat bike and plus tyre trail riding.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

secondary_chamber.name: IRT
secondary_chamber.note: IRT (Infinite Rate Tune) secondary positive air chamber. FILL IRT (TOP valve) FIRST, then main chamber (BOTTOM valve). IRT pressure = Main + 68 PSI for Mattoc. Real-world: 84 kg (185 lbs) → 70 PSI main / 140 PSI IRT. IRT only affects mid-stroke to end-stroke. +10 PSI IRT = more progressive/DH feel; -10 PSI = softer trail feel. IRT must always be minimum 10 PSI above main chamber. IVA spacers available for additional volume tuning.

forks.setup_notes: 34mm stanchion trail fork with IRT. TWO valves — IRT top, main bottom. Fill IRT first. Main ≈ 1/3 rider weight in lbs (85 kg / 187 lbs → ~62 PSI starting; real-world ~70 PSI). IRT = Main + 68 PSI. IVA (Incremental Volume Adjuster) for progression tuning. Comp, Expert (TPC), and Pro (TPC+) variants. Travel adjustable in 10mm increments via internal spacer.

rebound.note: 10-click rebound. Clockwise = slower rebound. Start at 4–5 clicks for average rider.

compression.label: Positions 1–6 (VTT-6P)
compression.note: VTT-6P: 6-position compression lever. Position 1 = most open. Position 6 = firmest. Start at Position 2–3 for trail. TPC+ (Pro) adds separate LSC dial.

damping.lsc_note: Pro variant only: TPC+ adds external LSC adjuster (~8 clicks) for low-speed compression tuning independently of the 6-position lever.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

secondary_chamber.name: IRT
secondary_chamber.note: IRT (Infinite Rate Tune) secondary positive air chamber. FILL IRT (TOP valve) FIRST, then main chamber (BOTTOM valve). IRT pressure = Main + 30 PSI for Mezzer. Real-world (160mm): 68 kg → 52 PSI main / 84 PSI IRT; 72.6 kg → 56 PSI main / 85 PSI IRT; 77 kg → 60 PSI main / 90 PSI IRT; 81.6 kg → 64 PSI main / 92 PSI IRT. +10 PSI IRT for DH/progressive; -10 PSI for trail. IRT must always be min 10 PSI above main. IVA spacers available.

forks.setup_notes: 37mm stanchion flagship enduro fork. IRT system with TWO valves — always fill IRT (top) first. Main ≈ 1/3 rider weight in lbs; IRT = Main + 30 PSI. Expert variant: VTT-6P damper (6-click compression). Pro variant: adds HSC. Travel adjustable 140–180mm in 10mm steps via internal spacer. IVA system for volume tuning (similar to RockShox tokens). 27.5 and 29-inch wheel sizes available.

rebound.note: 10-click rebound adjuster. Clockwise = slower. Enduro fork — start at 5 clicks and adjust.

compression.label: Positions 1–6 (VTT-6P)
compression.note: VTT-6P: 6-position compression lever. Start at Position 2 for trail, Position 3 for enduro. Expert variant only. Pro adds TPC+ LSC dial.

damping.lsc_note: Pro variant: TPC+ adds separate LSC dial (~8 clicks). Allows fine-tuning compression feel without changing platform position.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

forks.setup_notes: Dirt jump, slopestyle and park fork. ABS+ compression with Jump Stack tuning. Lower sag target (15–20%) for predictable jump behaviour. Run higher pressure vs trail riding. Standard: 20×110mm axle; Pro: 15mm thru-axle. Travel adjustable 80–140mm (Pro model). No IRT system.

rebound.note: Smooth sweep rebound dial (no detent clicks). DJ/park fork — set rebound firm enough to not kick back on landings. Start at 70% of range toward slow.

compression.note: ABS+ internal shim stack — not externally adjustable. Jump Stack tuning for consistent compression on repeated impacts. Send to Manitou for revalving if behaviour is not suitable.

xc_trail.note: N/A — jump fork

all_mountain.note: N/A — jump fork

enduro_dh.note: N/A — jump fork

forks.setup_notes: DH-specific fork. Expert/Pro variants are air-sprung (50–90 PSI range); Comp is coil spring (see coil_spring_rates_lbsin for spring selection by weight). No IRT system on Dorado. ABS+ damper with external high/low-speed compression and rebound. 36mm stanchions. Pressure chart printed on lower leg on some model years.

rebound.note: 24-click rebound. DH fork — wide range for varied terrain. Start at 8–10 clicks and tune per track.

lsc.note: 15-click LSC. Start at mid-range (7–8 clicks). Adjust for pedalling efficiency vs sensitivity.

hsc.note: 15-click HSC. Controls big hits and drops. Start at 6–7 clicks. Increase for harder landings.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.setup_notes: Manitou shocks run lower pressure than Fox/RockShox — starting point ~66–70% of rider weight in lbs (vs 100% for most brands). For 75 kg (165 lbs): start at ~110–115 PSI. Sealed atmospheric negative spring. IPA compression platform (4-click). Infinity-adjustable rebound. King Can upgrade option adds 54% more air volume for a more linear spring curve.

rebound.note: Infinity-adjustable rebound dial (no click detents). Turn counter-clockwise for faster rebound, clockwise for slower. Start at centre position and adjust 1/4 turn at a time. Rebound is correctly set when the shock returns quickly but doesn't kick the rider forward.

compression.label: Positions 1–4 (IPA)
compression.note: IPA (Incremental Platform Adjust): 4-lever positions. 1 = fully open (most plush). 4 = firm platform. Start at Position 2 for trail, Position 3 for enduro. King Can upgrade reduces need for firmer positions.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.setup_notes: Premium Mara with King Can sealed atmospheric negative spring for improved small-bump sensitivity. Same lower-pressure starting point as Mara (~66–70% of weight in lbs). Known range: 100–155 PSI for 68–86 kg riders. Adds high-speed compression adjuster vs standard Mara. IPA 4-position compression. Available in 230×60mm, 230×65mm and other eyelet sizes. Weight ~425 g.

rebound.note: Infinity-adjustable rebound dial (no click detents). Turn counter-clockwise for faster rebound, clockwise for slower. Start at centre position and adjust 1/4 turn at a time. Rebound is correctly set when the shock returns quickly but doesn't kick the rider forward.

compression.label: Positions 1–4 (IPA)
compression.note: IPA (Incremental Platform Adjust): 4-lever positions. 1 = fully open (most plush). 4 = firm platform. Start at Position 2 for trail, Position 3 for enduro. King Can upgrade reduces need for firmer positions.

hsc.note: Mara Pro adds external HSC dial (6 clicks). Controls bottom-out harshness and big hits. Start at 2–3 clicks from open. Pro-only feature over standard Mara.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

shocks.setup_notes: Standard air shock with non-adjustable atmospheric negative spring (sealed between two oil seals). Known data: 77 kg (170 lbs) → 135 PSI achieves ~30% sag. IPA compression platform (4 clicks). Infinity-adjustable rebound. Available: 152×32, 216×63, 222×65mm. Optional King Can upgrade (+54% air volume = more linear, reduces PSI required). Remote-lockout compatible. Max 300 PSI.

rebound.note: Infinity-adjustable rebound dial (no click detents). Turn counter-clockwise for faster rebound, clockwise for slower. Start at centre position and adjust 1/4 turn at a time. Rebound is correctly set when the shock returns quickly but doesn't kick the rider forward.

compression.label: Positions 1–4 (IPA)
compression.note: IPA (Incremental Platform Adjust): 4-lever positions. 1 = fully open (most plush). 4 = firm platform. Start at Position 2 for trail, Position 3 for enduro. King Can upgrade reduces need for firmer positions.

xc_trail.note: Faster rebound, less compression — efficient on smoother terrain

all_mountain.note: Balanced baseline — good starting point for mixed terrain

enduro_dh.note: Slower rebound, more compression control — for aggressive/rough terrain

helper_functions.description: Pseudocode / reference logic for building the calculator
get_recommended_psi: 1. Look up brand → type (fork/shock) → model in the data
2. If formula_psi == 'weight_lbs': starting_psi = weight_kg * 2.2046 (round to nearest integer)
3. Otherwise, find the pressure_chart entry where min_kg <= weight_kg < max_kg
4. Interpolate: t = (weight_kg - min_kg) / (max_kg - min_kg); psi = min_psi + t * (max_psi - min_psi)
5. Round result to nearest 5 PSI for practical use
7. If secondary_chamber is present, also return secondary chamber recommendation:
- Öhlins RXF forks (factor): secondary_psi = recommended_psi * secondary_chamber.factor
- Manitou IRT forks (offset_psi): secondary_psi = recommended_psi + secondary_chamber.offset_psi
- DVO shocks (fixed range): secondary_psi = secondary_chamber.min_psi to secondary_chamber.max_psi
- Round all secondary values to nearest 5 PSI

sag_calculation.description: How to calculate sag from total travel

sources: https://support.rockshox.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412440335643 — RockShox Rear Shock Air Pressure FAQ
https://www.ohlins.com/storage/TTX-Air-OM — Öhlins TTX Air Owner's Manual
Community data from PinkBike, NSMB, Flow Mountain Bike, Blister Review, MTBR
